mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate environment is expansionary.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of May 2019 indicates easing trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns. The mechanism is threshold-activated and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when unemployment crosses this critical level.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of May 2019 remains below trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts simultaneously fall below 1.0 million units annualized. This joint deterioration in consumer and residential demand signals weakening aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 2% YoY AND HOUST < 1000 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 883k units as of February 2019 indicates housing weakness
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced corporate profitability. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.98 as of April 2019 near 2017 baseline
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100k per month while unemployment rate remains stable, indicating labor market slack emerging without immediate wage pressure. This interaction signals equity market resilience as inflation remains contained, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k AND UNRATE stable within 0.2pp
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with moderating labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI declines below 2.0% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains above 2.0%, creating real rate tightening that constrains equity valuations. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in disinflationary environments.
regime_hint: disinflationary environment with real rate tightening
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while retail sales remain positive, indicating demand composition shift toward services and away from goods. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as service-sector equities outperform in low-growth regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 0%
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 101.0 while payroll employment growth exceeds 150k per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and strong corporate earnings growth. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both supply and labor inputs are expanding.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.98 and PAYEMS at 136.8M as of May 2019
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of instances when both INDPRO > 101.0 and PAYEMS growth > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
